steps.title: Isolate the b term
steps.content:
Start with the given equation and subtract $10$ from both sides:
$$
a=\frac{5}{13}b+10
$$
$$
a-10=\frac{5}{13}b
$$

steps.title: Solve for b
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by the reciprocal of $\frac{5}{13}$:
$$
b=\frac{13}{5}(a-10)
$$
Distribute the multiplier:
$$
b=\frac{13}{5}a-26
$$

steps.title: Match the expression
steps.content: The expression with coefficient $\frac{13}{5}$ and constant term $-26$ is the matching choice. The answer is D.
